name: basecamp
reason: Legacy exchanges a refresh token at Launchpad using type=refresh (not grant_type=refresh_token/client_credentials); oauth2_client_credentials hardcodes grant_type=client_credentials+client_id/secret (connsdk/auth.go:123); extra_params only ADDS keys, cannot rename grant_type or add refresh_token.

name: bunny-inc
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S3/S4 engine mini-waves did not add this capability). bunny-inc's legacy connector talks to a single per-tenant GraphQL endpoint (POST /graphql) with a query-document + variables JSON body, and paginates via an in-body cursor (data.<field>.pageInfo.{endCursor,hasNextPage}) read from the POST response, not a query-string/header pagination scheme the engine's declarative dialect models. Evidence: internal/connectors/engine/read.go's readOneSequence (the entire declarative stream-read path) always issues rt.Requester.Do(ctx, method, reqPath, query, nil) — the body argument is hardcoded nil on every read; StreamSpec.Body (bundle.go's `json:"body,omitempty"` field, commented "POST-body streams") is declared in the Go type but never read anywhere in read.go, i.e. dead/unwired. body_type (json/form) exists only on the WRITE path (write.go's buildBody/DoForm), never on stream reads. None of PaginationSpec's 6 types (none/link_header/page_number/offset_limit/cursor/next_url, engine/bundle.go:156) can express a POST-body GraphQL cursor read at all -- cursor pagination only reads a next-token from a GET/POST response BODY it already issued with a query-string request, never re-encodes a cursor into a subsequent request's OWN body. A Tier-1 (declarative JSON) expression is impossible; this is exactly the kind of protocol mismatch (in-body cursor pagination for a POST-only GraphQL endpoint) conventions.md section 1 lists as a StreamHook-caliber Tier-2 trigger, but a StreamHook alone cannot fix it either -- it would need to reimplement the entire request/pagination sequence in Go, which is explicitly the retired anti-pattern conventions.md's fan_out addition targeted; a genuine Tier-3 native-package rewrite is disproportionate for a single-endpoint GraphQL source. ENGINE_GAP: the dialect needs a POST-body-with-in-body-cursor-pagination primitive (or a graphql pagination type) before this connector can migrate.

name: canny
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S3/S4 engine mini-waves did not add this capability). Canny's documented API (developers.canny.io) requires every list endpoint to be called as POST with the secret apiKey AND pagination params (limit/skip) carried in a form-encoded request body -- never a header or query string (legacy canny.go's harvest: r.DoForm(ctx, http.MethodPost, endpoint.resource, nil, form) where form carries apiKey/limit/skip). Evidence: internal/connectors/engine/read.go's readOneSequence (the entire declarative stream-read path) always issues rt.Requester.Do(ctx, method, reqPath, query, nil) -- the body argument is hardcoded nil on every read, and it never calls connsdk.Requester.DoForm (http.go:187) at all; DoForm is only ever invoked from the WRITE path (write.go:211). StreamSpec.Body (bundle.go's `json:"body,omitempty"` field) is declared but never read in read.go -- dead/unwired for reads. Beyond the missing body-send mechanism, canny's own auth (the secret apiKey traveling in the POST form body, never a header/query param/Authorization scheme) doesn't match any of engine/auth.go's AuthSpec modes (none/bearer/basic/api_key_header/api_key_query/oauth2_client_credentials/custom) either -- api_key_query only ever adds the key to the query STRING (auth.go), never a form body. Pagination itself (limit/skip offset, PaginationSpec's offset_limit type) is otherwise expressible, but the secret and the offset/limit params are unreachable without a body-carrying request, so the gap is load-bearing, not cosmetic. ENGINE_GAP: the dialect needs a form-body-carries-auth-and-pagination-params primitive on the read path before this connector can migrate.

name: fastbill
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S4 engine mini-wave's fan_out/keyed_object/start_page:0/extra_params/date-only additions do not touch this gap). FastBill's only endpoint (.../api/1.0/api.php) is a single POST with a JSON body carrying SERVICE (e.g. "customer.get") plus LIMIT/OFFSET pagination fields; records come back nested under RESPONSE.<COLLECTION> (legacy: internal/connectors/fastbill/fastbill.go's harvest/serviceBody). Evidence: internal/connectors/engine/read.go's readOneSequence always issues rt.Requester.Do(ctx, method, reqPath, query, nil) -- the body argument is hardcoded nil on every declarative read, and StreamSpec.Body (bundle.go's `json:"body,omitempty"` field, commented "POST-body streams") is declared but never read anywhere in read.go -- dead/unwired. None of PaginationSpec's 6 types send pagination state via a request body (page_number/offset_limit only ever add query-string params). A Tier-1 expression is impossible without a body-carrying read primitive.

name: feishu
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S4 engine mini-wave's oauth2_client_credentials extra_params addition does not change this gap). Feishu/Lark authenticates via a tenant_access_token EXCHANGE: app_id + app_secret are POSTed as a JSON body to open-apis/auth/v3/tenant_access_token/internal, which returns {code, msg, tenant_access_token, expire} (a non-zero code is an API-level error even on HTTP 200); the token is cached with a TTL (legacy: internal/connectors/feishu/auth.go's tenantTokenAuth, ~2h default, refreshed 60s before expiry) and applied as a Bearer header. Evidence: engine/auth.go's only token-exchange auth mode is oauth2_client_credentials, whose connsdk.OAuth2ClientCredentials (connsdk/auth.go:122-138) hardcodes an application/x-www-form-urlencoded body with fixed field names grant_type/client_id/client_secret/scope (extra_params only ADDS keys, per S4, never renames these or switches encoding) and parses a standard access_token/expires_in JSON response -- incompatible with Feishu's JSON-body app_id/app_secret request and tenant_access_token/expire/code response shape on every axis (encoding, field names, response schema, and the code-in-200-body error convention). No other AuthSpec mode (none/bearer/basic/api_key_header/api_key_query/custom) fits either; mode custom would resolve via an AuthHook, which is a Tier-2 Go escape hatch, out of scope for a JSON-only bundle.

name: fillout
reason: PARTIAL (see internal/connectors/defs/fillout/): forms migrated to Tier-1 (no fan-out needed, GET /forms is a single unpaginated top-level array). questions and submissions remain quarantined -- confirmed still blocked after repair attempt now that fan_out exists (S4 engine mini-wave). Both require per-form sub-resource fan-out where legacy's resolveFormIDs (internal/connectors/fillout/fillout.go:259) uses the configured comma-separated form_id WHEN SET, else discovers every form id via GET /forms -- a runtime conditional fallback between two id sources. Evidence: FanOutIDsFrom (engine/bundle.go:243) and resolveFanOutIDs (engine/read.go:122-138) require declaring EXACTLY ONE of config_key or request at load time (both or neither is a read-time error) -- there is no combinator for "config_key when present, else request". Statically choosing request would silently return every form's data for a caller who set form_id to narrow scope (a legacy-accepted input emitting MORE records than legacy); statically choosing config_key would silently emit ZERO records for a caller who left form_id unset (legacy's other accepted, and most common, input, emitting FEWER records than legacy). Both diverge from legacy for a legacy-accepted input, which conventions.md section 5's meta-rule forbids as a silent workaround -- a narrower, fan_out-specific gap than the pre-S4 quarantine reason (which predated fan_out's existence entirely).

name: free-agent-connector
reason: RE-CONFIRMED after repair attempt now that fan_out/keyed_object/start_page:0/oauth2 extra_params/date-only bounds all exist (S4 engine mini-wave) -- none of those close this gap; it is purely an auth-mode gap. Legacy authenticates via OAuth2 grant_type=refresh_token against /token_endpoint, with the client authenticated via HTTP BASIC (client_id:client_secret, req.SetBasicAuth) rather than form-embedded credentials, plus refresh_token in the form body (internal/connectors/free-agent-connector/free_agent_connector.go:263-335). The engine's only declarative server-side-token-exchange auth mode, oauth2_client_credentials (engine/auth.go:100-146), performs solely a client-credentials grant (grant_type=client_credentials, client_id/client_secret always sent as FORM fields) and has no refresh_token-grant variant at all -- auth.go's mode switch (auth.go:64-108) recognizes exactly none/bearer/basic/api_key_header/api_key_query/oauth2_client_credentials/custom, with no 8th declarative mode for this grant type. This IS expressible via a Tier-2 custom AuthHook (docs/migration/conventions.md section 1's listed "token-exchange auth" trigger; internal/connectors/hooks/strava/hooks.go and hooks/gmail already port the near-identical refresh-token-grant shape) -- a hooks/free-agent-connector/hooks.go of ~90 lines adapting strava's pattern for HTTP Basic client auth would fully unblock this connector. This task's own scope is restricted to JSON+docs.md only (no Go hook files); the fresh, narrower blocker is therefore "unblockable within a JSON-only migration pass," not "unblockable by the engine" -- a follow-up pass authorized to add internal/connectors/hooks/free-agent-connector/hooks.go (Tier-2, single AuthHook interface, well under the 300-line soft cap) should close this without any further engine change.

name: google-analytics-data-api
reason: RE-CONFIRMED after repair attempt now that fan_out/keyed_object/start_page:0/oauth2 extra_params/date-only bounds all exist (S4 engine mini-wave) -- none of those close this gap; it is a POST-body-read gap, structurally untouched by any of them. GA4 Data API has no REST list endpoints: every report is a POST /v1beta/properties/{id}:runReport call whose request body itself carries offset/limit pagination state and a dimension/metric query (internal/connectors/google-analytics-data-api/google_analytics_data_api.go:268-286), and each response row is a positional dimensionValues/metricValues array projected against dimensionHeaders/metricHeaders (report.go:57-70) -- neither shape has a Tier-1 equivalent. Confirmed by direct engine read: StreamSpec.Body (engine/bundle.go:209) is a DECLARED-BUT-DEAD JSON field -- grep across internal/connectors/engine/read.go shows every declarative read call (read.go:207, read.go:327) invokes rt.Requester.Do(ctx, method, path, query, nil) with a hard-coded nil body, so a bundle-declared "body" map is never sent regardless of what streams.json contains; there is no pagination type that advances an offset/limit INSIDE a request body (all 6 documented types + none read next-page state from query params, headers, or response body, never write it into a follow-up body); and RecordsSpec/keyed_object's positional-array-projected-against-a-parallel-header-array shape (GA4's dimensionValues[i] <-> dimensionHeaders[i].name) has no dialect equivalent either (keyed_object flattens a map keyed by id, not a positional index-matched pair of arrays). This is exactly the "StreamHook whose real wire shape has no declarative equivalent for the replay server to match against at all" case conventions.md section 4 names as the sanctioned skip-marker scenario -- but even the StreamHook escape hatch itself requires a Go hook file, which this task's JSON+docs.md-only scope forecloses. A follow-up pass authorized to add Go (either a StreamHook Tier-2 hook overriding the whole runReport-driven read, or a genuine Tier-1 dialect extension adding POST-body templating + positional row projection) is required; no further JSON-only repair attempt will change this outcome.

reason: Legacy uses a custom session-token exchange auth flow: POST {email,password,strategy:'local'} JSON body to /authentication, read accessToken from the response, cache it, and inject it RAW (no 'Bearer ' prefix) into every subsequent Authorization header (internal/connectors/hoorayhr/hoorayhr.go:242-2

name: ip2whois
reason: UPDATED (wave3 unblock, superseding the prior reason below): the original per-domain-request-iteration + whole-object fan-out blocker is now solved by the fan_out dialect (fan_out.ids_from.config_key over the comma-separated domains config value) -- whois and 4 per-role contact streams (contacts_registrant/admin/tech/billing) are migrated to internal/connectors/defs/ip2whois (status: partial). Only the nameservers stream remains blocked: legacy's nameserverRecords fans the raw nameservers field -- a bare JSON array of SCALAR strings, not objects -- out into one record per nameserver ({domain, nameserver}). Neither connsdk.RecordsAt (keeps only array elements that decode as a JSON object; a bare string element is silently dropped) nor records.keyed_object (explodes a JSON object's VALUES, which must themselves be objects) fans a scalar-valued array into one record per element; joining into a single string instead would change record cardinality versus legacy's genuine 1-record-per-nameserver stream, an accepted-input data change. See internal/connectors/defs/ip2whois/docs.md Known limits and conventions.md's parity-deviation ledger entry 12. Superseded original reason: legacy performs one HTTP lookup per domain in a config-supplied comma-separated domain list (N requests per stream read from one config value) and fans each single-object lookup response out into 1 (whois), 0..4 (contacts), or 0..N (nameservers) output records. Neither 'iterate a delimited config list' nor 'fan a single response into N streams' had a declarative primitive at the time this connector was first quarantined.

name: mode
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S4 engine mini-wave's fan_out/keyed_object/start_page:0/oauth2 extra_params/date-only additions do not touch this gap). Mode's REST API is HAL+JSON: list responses embed records under _embedded.<resource> and the next page is followed via _links.next.href (legacy internal/connectors/mode/mode.go's harvest loop, and its own mode_test.go's TestReadPaginatesAndAuthenticates fixture, which serves a RELATIVE next link, "/acme/spaces?page=2", and legacy's own doc comment: "The href may be a relative path-with-query or an absolute URL; either is fed straight back to the Requester, which treats absolute URLs as-is and resolves relative ones against base_url"). The engine's pagination.type: next_url paginator (engine/paginate.go's nextURL.Next -> checkOrigin) cannot follow this: checkOrigin parses the resolved next-page value with net/url and hard-rejects it whenever u.Host == "" ("next URL ... has no host; rejecting (fail closed)") -- confirmed directly: url.Parse("/acme/spaces?page=2").Host is the empty string, so a purely relative href fails the same-origin SSRF guard on the very first follow attempt, before a second page is ever requested. This is a genuine same-host-absolute-URL-only limitation, not a config/fixture issue: engine/read.go's readOneSequence passes page.URL straight to rt.Requester.Do with no relative-to-base_url resolution step (unlike legacy's own connsdk.Requester, which does resolve a relative next-link against BaseURL). No other pagination type in PaginationSpec (none/link_header/page_number/offset_limit/cursor) reads a next-page URL from the response body at all, so there is no Tier-1 substitute shape either. A Tier-2 StreamHook could reimplement the relative-href follow directly, but this task's scope is restricted to JSON+docs.md only (no Go hook files); the fresh, narrower blocker is therefore "the next_url paginator cannot resolve a relative href against base_url," distinct from (and more precise than) the original quarantine reason's framing.

name: my-hours
reason: Confirmed still blocked after repair attempt (S4 engine mini-wave's fan_out/keyed_object/start_page:0/oauth2 extra_params/date-only additions do not touch this gap; it is purely an auth-shape gap). Legacy authenticates by POSTing a JSON body {email, password, grantType:"password", clientId:"api"} plus a custom api-version header to tokens/login, and caches the returned {accessToken: "..."} field as a Bearer token (internal/connectors/my-hours/my-hours.go's loginAuthenticator.accessToken). The engine's only declarative server-side-token-exchange auth mode, oauth2_client_credentials (engine/auth.go:100-146, connsdk/auth.go's OAuth2ClientCredentials), is incompatible on every axis: it hardcodes a form-urlencoded (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) body with fixed field names grant_type=client_credentials/client_id/client_secret/scope (connsdk/auth.go:122-133) -- extra_params (S4) only ADDS keys to this form, it cannot rename grant_type to "password", switch the encoding to JSON, rename client_id/client_secret to email/password, or add a clientId literal field; it also has no mechanism to attach a custom header (api-version) to the token-exchange request itself; and it parses a fixed access_token (snake_case) response field (connsdk/auth.go:156), never the camelCase accessToken shape My Hours actually returns. No other AuthSpec mode (none/bearer/basic/api_key_header/api_key_query/custom) fits a dynamic login-token-exchange either; mode custom would resolve via a Tier-2 AuthHook (engine/auth.go's buildCustomAuth), which is a Go escape hatch this task's JSON+docs.md-only scope forecloses. A follow-up pass authorized to add internal/connectors/hooks/my-hours/hooks.go (Tier-2, single AuthHook interface, porting the existing loginAuthenticator almost verbatim) would fully unblock this connector.
